description: SIP reply codes seen by OpenSIPS, broken down by Global, Gateway, Customer, and Route. A multi-select 'SIP Reply Code' filter at the top scopes every panel to the chosen code(s). Each section shows replies by SIP class, six frequency-banded per-code timeseries, and a detail table.

panels.title: GLOBAL
panels.description: All SIP reply codes received/sent by OpenSIPS, summed across the entire platform. Use the 'SIP Reply Code' selector at the top to scope the view to one or more specific codes.

panels.title: Global -- Replies per Second (all codes)
panels.description: Every reply code matched by the 'SIP Reply Code' filter as its own line. Use this panel to eyeball the full spread at once; the six frequency-banded panels below zoom into codes currently at each rate tier.

panels.title: Global -- trace ( > 0, < 0.1 /s )
panels.description: Per-code reply rates currently in the trace band: extremely rare codes (>0, <0.1 /s). Occasional blips. A sustained line here may indicate a newly-woken code.

panels.title: Global -- rare ( 0.1 - 1 /s )
panels.description: Per-code reply rates currently in the rare band: rare codes (0.1-1 /s). Low-frequency replies that vanish in the total. A code promoted here from trace is starting to recur.

panels.title: Global -- low ( 1 - 5 /s )
panels.description: Per-code reply rates currently in the low band: low-rate codes (1-5 /s). Persistent background. Newcomers from rare are mild but worth investigating.

panels.title: Global -- moderate ( 5 - 25 /s )
panels.description: Per-code reply rates currently in the moderate band: moderate-rate codes (5-25 /s). Steady reply sources. Anything entering this band from below signals a real shift.

panels.title: Global -- high ( 25 - 100 /s )
panels.description: Per-code reply rates currently in the high band: high-rate codes (25-100 /s). Loud responses that need investigation unless they are 200/18x on the success path.

panels.title: Global -- storm ( >= 100 /s )
panels.description: Per-code reply rates currently in the storm band: storm-rate codes (>=100 /s). Severe failures unless this is the normal success-path volume (200_INVITE, 183_INVITE, 200_BYE).

panels.title: Global -- Reply Code Detail
panels.description: Per-reply-code table showing: Now (5m rate), Max 1h (worst rate in the past hour), 6h ago (rate at this time earlier today). Spot codes that just started firing (low Baseline + non-zero Now) or that are still elevated after a spike (Max 1h >> Now). Code column renders human descriptions via value mappings.

404_BYE.text: 404 BYE -- Not Found (stale dialog on BYE)

408_BYE.text: 408 BYE -- Request Timeout (peer unreachable on BYE)

panels.title: BY GATEWAY  --  $gw
panels.description: Filter by the 'Gateway' variable at the top to select a specific outbound gateway. The 'SIP Reply Code' selector scopes every panel to the chosen code(s) for that gateway.

panels.title: ${gw} -- Replies per Second (all codes)
panels.description: Every reply code matched by the 'SIP Reply Code' filter as its own line. Use this panel to eyeball the full spread at once; the six frequency-banded panels below zoom into codes currently at each rate tier.

panels.title: ${gw} -- trace ( > 0, < 0.1 /s )
panels.description: Per-code reply rates currently in the trace band: extremely rare codes (>0, <0.1 /s). Occasional blips. A sustained line here may indicate a newly-woken code.

panels.title: ${gw} -- storm ( >= 100 /s )
panels.description: Per-code reply rates currently in the storm band: storm-rate codes (>=100 /s). Severe failures unless this is the normal success-path volume (200_INVITE, 183_INVITE, 200_BYE).

panels.title: ${gw} -- Reply Code Detail
panels.description: Per-reply-code table showing: Now (5m rate), Max 1h (worst rate in the past hour), 6h ago (rate at this time earlier today). Spot codes that just started firing (low Baseline + non-zero Now) or that are still elevated after a spike (Max 1h >> Now). Code column renders human descriptions via value mappings.

panels.title: BY CUSTOMER  --  $cu
